question: Can a true monthly enabled-vs-absent control surface be honestly derived from the current retained repo-visible artifacts, or is monthly_general_control_surface still blocked by a material artifact gap?

repo_visible_artifact_scan.interpretation: No repo-visible retained monthly artifact with monthly+enabled+absent naming was found under results/** during this feasibility slice.

blocked_because: The current canonical month ranking surface is not an enabled-vs-absent surface; it is continuation_release_hysteresis baseline vs release_zero.
Canonical Edge Topology artifacts from Phase 2 and Phase 3 still mark the general monthly enabled-vs-absent layer as unsupported/unknown on the current retained monthly surface.
Phase 5 and Phase 6 already classify monthly_general_control_surface as UNKNOWN for this exact surface-gap reason.
The repo-visible retained results scan found no monthly enabled-vs-absent artifact family under results/**.

topology_implications: monthly_general_control_surface should remain UNKNOWN on the current retained Edge Topology lane.
The blocker is a material artifact-gap blocker, not just interpretive caution.
The current monthly seam inventory must not be re-labeled as a general monthly control map.
Closing this gap requires an explicit new materialization slice, not more reinterpretation of the current seam evidence.

not_promoted_because: No retained repo-visible monthly enabled-vs-absent control surface currently exists for the lane to cite.
The current monthly evidence remains comparator-family specific and locally scoped.

next_admissible_follow_up: Only reopen monthly_general_control_surface with an explicit bounded slice that materializes or validates a true monthly enabled-vs-absent control surface, or proves an honest bridge without changing comparator semantics.
